publicEvidence: Public listing showed Business Site URL
Direct fetch returned HTTP 404 with Google 404 title
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: A public listing website click can hit a 404 before a tree-service customer reaches a phone/service path.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Replace dead Business Site path with mobile-first call/request page using verified phone and owner-approved proof.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Concrete public bug, urgent/helpful angle, easy for the owner to verify.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: Warm green field-card/tree-ring concept focused on dead listing path repair rather than dark hero/form/cards pattern.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: Browser visual QA said the page looked tailored and non-generic; patched CTA wording and repeated sample-only disclosure near hero.

draftCopy.phoneScript: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was checking local tree-service listings around Creston and noticed the public website link I found for Huddleson Tree Service opened to a Google 404 page. I did not want to assume you already knew, so I made a no-obligation sample showing how that dead click could become a simple call-first page using only verified public info. Worth sending the link over for review? If not, just say no and I will not follow up.

emailIfRouteVerified.subject: quick 404 note for Huddleson Tree Service
emailIfRouteVerified.body:
Hi Huddleson Tree Service team — quick helpful note, not a pitch blast. While reviewing local tree-service listings, I found a public website link for Huddleson that opened to a Google 404 page instead of a working page.

If customers click that from search, they may miss the phone/service path before choosing who to call. I made a no-obligation sample concept showing a cleaner first screen with your public phone and a simple tree-service intake path:
https://sybeorg-mockups.vercel.app/huddleson-tree-service/

Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicEvidence: No website field found in listing pass
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: Customers only see listing-level info and must infer plumbing/welding services, coverage, and call preparedness.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: One-page job-ticket site with tap-to-call, verified hours, and owner-approved service lines.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: No-site gap is obvious; small controlled page is low-friction.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: Blueprint/job-ticket page for rural plumbing and welding calls, not a generic contractor landing page.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: Browser visual QA positive; patched headline to include welding and added hero-area sample-only disclosure.

draftCopy.phoneScript: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was reviewing Waverly-area plumbing listings and found Davidson Plumbing & Welding with phone and hours, but I did not find an owned website field in the public listing I checked. I made a no-obligation sample of a simple job-ticket page that could help callers see what details to have ready before calling. Who is the best person to send that to? If it is not relevant, just say no and I will not follow up.

publicEvidence: No website field found in listing pass
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: Local searchers rely on listing data instead of a controlled well/plumbing service page.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Small local profile or one-page site focused on well/plumbing calls, hours, and service context.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Clear no-website/local-profile gap, but outreach should not lead with a second similar mockup today.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup created; would need confirmed service lines and contact route to avoid generic repetition.

draftCopy.phoneScript: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was reviewing Waverly-area plumbing and well listings and noticed Waverly Plumbing & Well had phone/hours listed, but I did not find an owned website field in the public listing I checked. I had a small idea for a one-page local profile that makes the phone path and well/plumbing service context clearer. Who should I send that idea to? Replying no is totally fine and I will not follow up.

publicEvidence: Title: Bohl Plumbing & Heating, Inc. - Nebraska City, NE
Homepage fetch found forms and tel links
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: Existing site can send high-intent visitors into a broad homepage instead of a single trackable service-call path.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Campaign-specific page for emergency plumbing/heating, water heater, or furnace requests after service focus is chosen.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Less threatening than redesign pitch; uses existing credibility and offers a narrow revenue path.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup today; would need selected service-line and owner-approved offer to avoid generic landing page.

email.subject: focused service-page idea for Bohl Plumbing & Heating
email.body:
Hi Bohl Plumbing & Heating team — I came across your Nebraska City site today. This is not a “you need a new website” note; your site already loads and has contact paths.

The opportunity I noticed is more specific: if you ever run seasonal or high-intent traffic for plumbing/heating calls, a dedicated landing page could keep visitors on one action — call or request service — instead of sending them through the broader homepage.

I can put together a no-obligation sample around one service line using only details you approve. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicEvidence: Title: Kvale Tree Service - Eagle Grove, IA
Homepage fetch found forms and tel links
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: Tree-service visitors with storm urgency need a clearer one-action path than a general site flow.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Storm cleanup or hazardous-limb page using real photos/proof and approved service categories.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Seasonal helpful angle, reachable public email, and not a generic rebuild pitch.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup today; useful concept needs real photos/proof and confirmed storm service emphasis.

email.subject: storm cleanup page idea for Kvale Tree Service
email.body:
Hi Kvale Tree Service team — I was reviewing Iowa tree-service pages and noticed your site/contact paths are already live, so I would not lead with a generic redesign pitch.

The specific idea is a focused storm cleanup / hazardous-limb page that keeps mobile visitors on one next step: call, send photos, or request help. It could use your real photos and approved service details rather than generic tree-service claims.

Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.
